name: Buddy Williams
abstract: Ira "Buddy" Williams (born December 17, 1952, in New York City) is an American jazz drummer. He has played with Grover Washington, Cedar Walton, David Sanborn, Kirk Whalum, Joe Sample, The Manhattan Transfer, Nat Adderley, Roberta Flack, McCoy Tyner and others.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Tribute to Sarah Vaughan
Rainforest Foundation Benefit Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: The New York Pops: Forever Broadway - Celebrating Cy Coleman

works.t: It Started With a Dream: I'm Gonna Laugh You Right Out of My Life

about_quotes.speaker: John LaPorta
about_quotes.evidence: It was a band in Philadelphia by the name of Buddy Williams that, he got stuck for lead alto and he asked me about it... And started playing lead alto for him and then kept it up for twenty years.
about_quotes.doc_title: John LaPorta interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: All the way up my band, the original Untouchables, Larry Marino, Charlie Burbank, and Dalton Russo, Buddy Williams, all these people could play.

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing, warming up, I was in the corner of Saturday Night Live and Buddy was on the other side of the room at the drumset. And it’s a simple tune... I realized on the other side of the room I’m hearing [scats drumming]. Buddy’s already got it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Walter "Furry" Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: We had me, Smiley, and Dan Spiegel, and Bill Allen, and Joe Harris... and then, uh, Mr. Five, and the boy, Buddy Williams, when he's drunk.

about_quotes.speaker: James "Sugarboy" Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: Buddy Williams played drums. Frank Field was playing bass. Smiley Lewis was playing guitar.

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: I was asked by a very good friend and drummer Buddy Williams, to come into the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
